title: Behavioral eval harness: scored conversation + recall scenarios, regression-gated
current_state: The coherence gate proves turns aren't DROPPED; nothing scores whether Pi answers WELL — recall accuracy, contradiction rate, tool-choice correctness have no benchmark. Every horizon (autonomy, memory v2, normie tools) changes behavior with no behavioral measuring stick. T-152's lesson: emergent properties need their own harness.
target_state: testing/evals/: scenario files (YAML/JSON) = scripted multi-turn conversations against a seeded memory fixture with EXPECTED behaviors (must-mention facts, must-not-contradict, must-call-tool X / must-not-call). A runner executes them offline against fake providers where possible and scores deterministically (substring/structural checks — no LLM-judge in v1). Costly live variant (real cheap-tier) runs manually like other COSTLY_TESTS. Score history appended to logs/evals.jsonl; retro.py charts the trend; a hard floor on the offline subset joins verify.py once stable.
migration_plan: 1. Scenario schema + 10 seed scenarios from REAL past failures: T-148 context drop, T-160 location contradiction, T-146 wrong-city weather, normie miming, prefetch recall (T-151), mode-switch context survival (L-001).
2. Runner: seeded temp SQLite + scripted fake provider responses where determinism is needed; live cheap-tier mode behind --live.
3. Scoring: deterministic checks only in v1 (presence/absence/ordering).
4. verify.py: offline eval subset as a soft report first, hard gate after 2 stable weeks.
5. Every future behavioral bug adds a scenario in its fix PR (the SOLUTIONS pattern, executable).
risk_notes: Keep v1 deterministic and offline — an LLM-judge eval would be flaky in CI (exactly the T-175 class). Scenarios-from-real-failures is the rule; invented scenarios test imagination, not Pi.
